method_atom: An exact trace formula that expresses the discrete eigenvalue spectrum of a wave operator defined on a network of one-dimensional segments joined at nodes as a sum over the closed orbits of an associated classical dynamics; the associated area-preserving classical map is mixing with periodic orbits proliferating exponentially, and the resulting spectral fluctuation statistics are shown to coincide with those of a random-matrix ensemble. The machinery links a discrete spectrum, its periodic-orbit expansion, and universal random-matrix statistics as a testbed for the correspondence between spectral universality and chaotic underlying dynamics.
object_atom: The quantum energy spectrum of a particle propagating along the bonds of a finite metric graph (a network of bonds and vertices), together with the associated classical ray dynamics of bond-to-bond scattering, studied in the field of quantum chaos.
brief_reason: What carries across is the trace-formula / periodic-orbit-to-random-matrix machinery on a mixing dynamical system; what is domain-specific is the quantized metric graph and its quantum-chaos spectrum, and the two separate cleanly.
